current_judgment: Prompt updated (2026-03-21) to add explicit dimension definitions including a POV-fidelity clause in voice_consistency. Re-tested qwen2.5:14b against 4 scenes (scene-04 plus 3 new POV validation scenes). Results are partial: scene-04 improved from 0.85 to 0.60 (model now correctly attributes the issue to voice_consistency in text, but score remains above ≤0.50 threshold). scene-08-pov-clean correctly scored 1.00 (no false positive). However, scene-06-pov-hard (reader-address POV break) and scene-07-pov-subtle (omniscient slip) both scored 1.00 — entirely missed. Two distinct failure modes identified: (1) narrator-to-reader direct address not recognized as a POV violation; (2) single-sentence omniscient aside in limited-third not flagged. The prompt definition covers these cases in principle but the model is not applying them. Further prompt refinement is required.
next_required_decision: Prompt fix for voice_consistency is partial. scene-04 improved (0.85 to 0.60) but did not reach the ≤0.50 threshold. scene-06 (reader-address POV break) and scene-07 (omniscient slip in limited-third) both scored 1.00 — the updated definition was insufficient to catch these structurally different POV violations. Two further refinements are needed: (1) add explicit examples of narrator-to-reader direct address as a POV violation to the voice_consistency definition; (2) add explicit mention that a single omniscient sentence embedded in limited-third POV is a voice consistency failure. Re-test scenes 06 and 07 against the further-revised prompt. Once all 4 POV validation scenes pass their thresholds, run the full 5-scene baseline set against the updated prompt to confirm no regressions, then assess candidate_baseline readiness.
provenance_notes: Metrics derived from operator judgment across 5 eval scenes (run-2026-03-21-001 through run-2026-03-21-005). schema_reliability: 5/5 runs returned valid schema envelope. false_positive_rate: 0 clearly incorrect findings identified across all 5 runs. surface_detection_rate: 4/4 targeted style issues detected (scene-04 counted as detected despite dimension miscategorization). All metrics are operator-judged from a small 5-scene set. Not benchmark-derived. Not gate-eligible. metric_profile is set to detection_reasoning as the closest available value; style analysis identifies issues and reasons about them but is not a binary detection task.
